mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 8.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 230.0 indicates accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fail to generate positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and dividend payouts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 90.0 while retail sales remain stable signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of months where INDPRO falls below 90.0.
